summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined several specific developments in small UAS operations. Ukraine has reportedly destroyed a Russian Malva self-propelled gun using an FPV drone, and set a world record with an FPV strike on a Russian warship at over 350 kilometers. Ukrainian forces are testing FPV drones specifically targeting anti-drone electronic warfare systems, while domestically produced unjammable drones are leaving wires across battlefields, forcing troops to move with caution. Reports indicate that fiber optic drones are flooding the battlefield from the Russian side, prompting Ukraine to race to develop comparable systems. Additionally, Ukrainian companies are manufacturing exploding unjammable drones designed to counter Russian electronic warfare, and the conflict has spurred the emergence of hit-to-kill drone intercepts as a new form of aerial combat.
